run.focus: operational patterns from parallel professional disciplines

findings.title: Silent partial review: provider outage looks identical to complete review
findings.fix: Add explicit fallback routing: if OpenRouter backend fails for an agent, re-route to Claude haiku and surface provider_fallback warning to user. Must never be silent.

findings.title: No signal quality monitoring: shallow cheap-model output indistinguishable from thorough review
findings.fix: In synthesis, compute findings-density check per cheap-model agent: if finding count >50% below historical median for that agent type, flag low_signal_confidence and weight findings lower in convergence scoring.

findings.title: Single-source P0 findings from cheap models accepted without verification escalation
findings.fix: In synthesis, flag any P0/P1 finding that is single-source AND from a provider below top quality tier. Surface verification_recommended prominently. Optionally auto-escalate to Claude verification agent.

findings.title: No post-routing analysis: efficiency gains unverifiable after shipping
findings.fix: Add routing_report block to synthesis output: per-provider {model, tokens, cost, findings_count, severity_distribution}. Data already flows through synthesis — one additional output block.

findings.title: No model qualification process: cheap models added without quality baseline
findings.fix: Define qualification checklist: 3 synthetic review tasks with known findings, measure format compliance + precision/recall vs Claude baseline. Store in config/flux-drive/model-registry.yaml. Requalify on major model updates.

findings.title: Convergence scoring treats all providers equally: cross-family agreement not distinguished from same-family
findings.fix: Add provider_family field to findings. Weight convergence scores by provider diversity: cross-family agreement scores 1.5x vs same-family agreement.

findings.title: Provider failover is manual: no automatic switching on API failure
findings.fix: Add retry-with-escalation in OpenRouter dispatch wrapper: 2 retries with backoff, then re-dispatch to Claude haiku. Log provider_fallback: true.

findings.title: Quality feedback loop absent: routing system cannot learn from outcomes
findings.fix: Design feedback loop architecture before shipping: even manual reporting loop closes the gap. estimate-costs.sh --routing-report output per-provider quality/cost metrics.

findings.title: Total cost of ownership ignores hidden costs: retry overhead, validation, synthesis rework
findings.fix: Add TCO fields to budget.yaml documentation: retry_cost_multiplier, validation_overhead_tokens, expected_quality_discount.

findings.title: Format conversion artifacts: different model families use different severity label conventions
findings.fix: Add findings format normalizer in post-processing before synthesis ingestion. Map severity label variants (CRITICAL→P0, HIGH→P1, etc.) to canonical format. Log normalization events.

findings.title: Claude verification applied uniformly: not concentrated on high-uncertainty findings
findings.fix: Add uncertainty escalation to synthesis: high-uncertainty findings (single-source P0, cross-provider divergence) routed to Claude verification agent as Stage 2 expansion. Pre-allocate 20% of budget for escalation pool.

findings.title: Cost-quality coupling unacknowledged: no explicit break-even threshold for routing eligibility
findings.fix: Define explicit routing eligibility threshold: agent type X routes to cheap model only if historical finding density on cheap model is within N% of Claude baseline. Without this, optimization has no stopping condition.

findings.title: Order splitting ignores workload divisibility: routing by agent type is a proxy for a better signal
findings.fix: Extend triage complexity signal (already exists) to gate model tier selection: large complexity + cross-cutting agent → Claude required.

findings.title: Cross-provider convergence is emergent: diversity conditions not designed for
findings.fix: Add independence validation to model qualification: measure Claude vs cheap model agreement rate per finding type. If >90% agreement, models are not independent for that type — no diversity benefit.

cross_agent_convergences.theme: Provider outage / quality degradation silent failure
cross_agent_convergences.note: Same underlying gap (no monitoring), different failure modes (outage vs degradation)
TCA-01 (no post-routing analysis) + SYS-01 (no quality feedback loop) are two expressions of the same gap
NDT-02 (convergence weighting) + SYS-03 (emergence conditions) frame the same insight from different directions
